subject: Project Management
title: Dubai's Palm Islands
description: A short lesson to help you apply Dubai's Palm Islands.

hook.text: Dubai built an island shaped like a palm tree — visible from space — entirely from dredged sand.

buildup.text: 94 million cubic meters of sand were sprayed into the sea by a fleet of dredging ships.

discovery.text: A 10.5 km crescent-shaped breakwater protects the island from waves — built rock by rock.

twist.text: The island is slowly sinking. Engineers constantly monitor settlement and reinforce foundations.

climax.text: Palm Jumeirah added 78 km of coastline to a desert city — an audacious bet on building land from sea.

punchline.text: They ran out of beach, so they built more ocean.

quiz.question: What is happening to Dubai's Palm Island over time?
options: It is slowly sinking and needs constant monitoring
